Home / Art Objects
The Forest Chalet

Art Objects

The interior of The Forest Chalet is enriched by a carefully curated collection of unique art objects, handcrafted furniture, and collectible design pieces that reflect the creative vision of its owners.

Among the most distinctive works is a handcrafted brass feature wall, designed and created by Sergey Dronov, which serves as the architectural focal point of the living space. Equally remarkable is the contemporary mixed-media installation “The Wedding of Peacocks”, combining hand-crocheted textile panels with painted elements into a single artistic composition.

Another signature work is the sculptural Lotus Throne, conceived and handcrafted by Sergey Dronov. Inspired by the geometry of lotus petals, it functions as both a piece of furniture and a sculptural artwork.

The interior also includes custom bedside lamps and a floor lamp made of polished stainless steel in the spirit of Mid-Century Modern design, together with a handcrafted copper kettle, a matching umbrella stand, and a minimalist Bauhaus candlestick.

Vintage and collectible furniture further defines the character of the chalet, including a striking red leather sofa from an experimental De Sede collection, a traditional Polish cast-iron stove, and a Spanish chandelier and wall sconces in the New Baroque style. Completing the ensemble is an elegant decorative mirror by EDG, bringing additional depth and light to the interior.

Together, these objects create an environment where contemporary art, handcrafted design, and carefully selected European furnishings coexist in harmony with the warmth of natural wood and the surrounding forest.

Fiber Art & Sculptural Objects

Hand-worked textile compositions, sculptural furniture and lighting extend the chalet's collection into the language of contemporary craft.

No. 01
“Wedding of Peacocks”
Hand-woven fiber art composition, per accompanying documentation
A multi-panel textile composition in crochet and rag-weave technique, developed as a series of concentric circular motifs across several framed panels.
No. 02
Leather Sofa
De Sede, Switzerland, per accompanying documentation
Red leather sofa by the Swiss manufacturer De Sede, shown beneath the “Wedding of Peacocks” panels.
No. 03
“Flower Field”
Hand-knitted textile collage, per accompanying documentation
A large fabric collage assembled from hand-knitted and crocheted floral fragments, combining velvet appliqué leaves with beaded and embroidered blossoms.
No. 04
Decorative Mirror & Wall Sconces
Mirror by EDG; sconces Spain, New Baroque style, per accompanying documentation
Decorative mirror by EDG in a hand-hammered brass frame with tile inlays, flanked by a pair of crystal wall sconces from Spain in the New Baroque style.
No. 05
Brass Feature Wall & Cast-Iron Stove
Brass wall by Sergey Dronov; stove Poland, per accompanying documentation
Handcrafted brass feature wall with a stylized bamboo-leaf motif, designed and made by Sergey Dronov, forming the architectural focal point of the living space beside a traditional Polish cast-iron stove.
No. 06
Stainless Steel Table Lamp
Mid-Century Modern style, per accompanying documentation
Custom table lamp in polished stainless steel with a wave-form stem, in the spirit of Mid-Century Modern design; a matching floor lamp is used elsewhere in the interior.
No. 07
Lotus Throne
Sergey Dronov, per accompanying documentation
Sculptural wooden throne conceived and handcrafted by Sergey Dronov, its geometry inspired by lotus petals, with a hand-painted lotus-flower panel and teal-and-gold decorative accents. Functions as both a piece of furniture and a sculptural artwork.
No. 08
Woven Textile Panel — Architectural Motif
Hand-crocheted fiber art, per accompanying documentation
Hand-crocheted textile panel with a striped, architectural column motif in blue, pink and cream tones, mounted beside the staircase.
No. 09
Woven Textile Panel — Striped Composition
Hand-crocheted fiber art, per accompanying documentation
Hand-crocheted textile panel in grey and olive tones with a textured striped central band and red crocheted flower appliqués at the corners.
No. 10
Hydrangea Panel
Nona Dronova, oil on panel, per accompanying documentation
Long horizontal painted panel of hydrangeas in blue, white and lilac, mounted above the sofa as a bedside-height decorative frieze.

More works from this collection will be added…
